message:
fix(e2e): use toIdx variable in tab order verification

Fresh eyes review fix:
- Variable toIdx was declared but never used
- Added missing assertion: verify "to" field comes after "sender" in tab order
- Updated comment to document expected order (threadId → sender → to)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0rs","title":"Docs: fix apps/web test run commands","description":"## Problem
Several vitest-based unit tests in `apps/web/src/lib/*.test.ts` have header comments suggesting running them with `bun test ...`.

## Why this matters
AGENTS.md is explicit: for the web app, tests must be run via `cd apps/web && bun run test` so Vitest + happy-dom are configured. Suggesting `bun test` causes confusing failures (e.g. "document is not defined") and makes local dev slower.

## Fix
Update the header comments to recommend:
- `cd apps/web && bun run test -- <file>`

## Acceptance
- No behavior changes; only comments.
- Grep for `Run with: bun test apps/web` returns nothing.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T06:30:51.92657549-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T06:49:08.996370501-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T06:49:08.996370501-05:00","close_reason":"Updated vitest header comments to use: cd apps/web && bun run test -- <file>","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0rs","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.7.4.1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T06:30:51.94956319-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-0s7","title":"Legacy: Build excerpt builder component (superseded)","description":"# Task

Create the excerpt builder that allows users to select text from documents and build cited excerpt blocks.

# User Flow

1. User browses a document (e.g., transcript)
2. User selects text
3. Selection popover appears: 'Add to excerpt'
4. Selection added to 'Excerpt Basket' (sidebar or drawer)
5. User can add multiple selections
6. User composes final excerpt with ordering and context
7. Export as formatted markdown for session kickoff

# Excerpt Basket

- List of selections
- Each shows: text snippet, source doc, §-anchor
- Drag to reorder
- Delete button
- 'Add note' for each selection

# Output Format

```markdown
## Context for Brenner Loop

### From §42: The choice of organism
> The choice of the experimental object remains one of the most important things...

### From §87: Abundance trick
> If you can find a protein that dominates...

### User note
[Additional context or question]
```

# Persistence

- Store selections in localStorage
- Clear on 'Start Session' or manual clear

# Integration

- Works within document viewer
- Selections persist across page navigations
- Basket accessible from any page

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Text selection triggers popover
- [ ] Selections added to basket
- [ ] Basket shows all selections with metadata
- [ ] Reordering works
- [ ] Export generates correct format
- [ ] Persists across navigation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:15:35.813553979-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T20:15:59.636868573-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.486732227-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.2.2 + brenner_bot-5so.2.4.1","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0s7","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:25.083902194-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","title":"Task: Coverage Threshold Enforcement (80%)","description":"# Goal
Raise test coverage thresholds to 80% and enforce per-file minimum coverage.

# Current State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (current)
coverage: {
  thresholds: {
    lines: 50,
    functions: 50,
    branches: 50,
    statements: 50,
  }
}
```

# Target State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (target)
coverage: {
  provider: 'v8',
  reporter: ['text', 'lcov', 'html', 'json'],
  reportsDirectory: './coverage',
  include: ['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx'],
  exclude: [
    'src/**/*.test.ts',
    'src/**/*.test.tsx',
    'src/__fixtures__/**',
    'src/types/**',
    '**/*.d.ts',
  ],
  thresholds: {
    // Global thresholds
    lines: 80,
    functions: 80,
    branches: 75,
    statements: 80,
    
    // Per-file enforcement
    perFile: true,
    
    // Critical files with higher thresholds
    'src/lib/artifact-merge.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
    'src/lib/delta-parser.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
  },
  
  // Fail CI on threshold violation
  skipFull: false,
  
  // Check for uncovered lines
  checkCoverage: true,
}
```

# CI Integration
Update GitHub Actions workflow:
```yaml
# .github/workflows/test.yml
- name: Run tests with coverage
  run: bun test --coverage
  
- name: Check coverage thresholds
  run: |
    # Parse coverage report and fail if below threshold
    bun run coverage:check
    
- name: Upload coverage to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: ./apps/web/coverage/lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
```

# Coverage Badge
Add to README:
```markdown
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot)
```

# Gradual Rollout Plan
1. Week 1: Set global to 60%, add perFile
2. Week 2: Raise to 70%, identify low-coverage files
3. Week 3: Raise to 80%, enforce on all files
4. Ongoing: Add per-file exceptions only with justification

# Coverage Exclusion Guidelines
Exclude from coverage (with comment justification):
- Type definitions (*.d.ts)
- Test files and fixtures
- Generated code
- Configuration files
- Development-only code

DO NOT exclude:
- Complex business logic
- Error handling paths
- Edge cases

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Global threshold at 80% lines
- [ ] Per-file enforcement enabled
- [ ] Critical files at 90%
- [ ] CI fails on threshold violation
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Coverage report uploaded to Codecov
- [ ] Exclusion list documented","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:54:32.108038217-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:43:54.141552999-05:00","labels":["testing infrastructure coverage ci"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0meb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:56:20.596496889-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ponv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:56:20.62687603-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-4ep3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:37:16.960318921-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5","title":"Spec: Experiment Result Classification and Interpretation","description":"# Goal
Define how experiment results are classified and interpreted into DELTAs.

# Two-Stage Process (Key Insight)

**Stage 1: Classification** (structured, can be assisted)
- What KIND of result is this?
- Pass/fail/error/metrics/observation/anomaly?

**Stage 2: Interpretation** (judgment, guidelines only)
- What does this mean for hypotheses?
- What DELTA should be created?

# Stage 1: Result Classification

## The Taxonomy (Restored)
```typescript
type ExperimentResultType = 
  | "pass"           // Test completed, outcome matches expectation
  | "fail"           // Test completed, outcome contradicts expectation
  | "error"          // Test couldn't complete (crash, timeout, missing deps)
  | "metrics"        // Quantitative measurements without clear pass/fail
  | "observation"    // Qualitative finding requiring interpretation
  | "anomaly";       // Unexpected result that doesn't fit any prediction

interface ClassifiedResult {
  experiment_id: string;
  test_id: string;
  
  // Classification
  result_type: ExperimentResultType;
  confidence: "high" | "medium" | "low";
  
  // Extracted data (type-specific)
  metrics?: Record<string, number>;
  observation?: string;
  error_message?: string;
  
  // Which hypotheses this relates to
  hypothesis_refs: string[];  // ["H1", "H2"]
}
```

## Classification CLI (Assistant, Not Automator)
```bash
# Suggest classification based on output
./brenner.ts experiment classify \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3

# Output:
# Result type: metrics (confidence: high)
# Extracted metrics:
#   bleu_vanilla: 0.42
#   bleu_rrp: 0.48
#   bleu_freq_penalty: 0.45
# 
# Related hypotheses: H1, H2 (based on test T1 definition)
# 
# Suggested interpretation pattern: "metrics_comparison"
# See: ./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern metrics_comparison
```

# Stage 2: Interpretation Patterns

Classification tells you WHAT you have. Interpretation decides WHAT IT MEANS.

## Pattern: pass → Predictions Update
**When**: Test passed, result matches hypothesis prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "predictions_table",
  "target_id": "P1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_H1": "Confirmed: 0.03 BLEU difference, within equivalence threshold"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 passed: equivalence holds at tested scale"
}
```

## Pattern: fail → Hypothesis Weakening
**When**: Test clearly contradicts a hypothesis
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "hypothesis_slate",
  "target_id": "H1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "weakened",
    "evidence_against": "T1 showed 15% quality gap at 16k context"
  },
  "rationale": "H1 predicted equivalence; T1 found significant divergence"
}
```

## Pattern: error → Test Blocked
**When**: Experiment couldn't complete
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "blocker": "OOM at 16k context, need 32GB instance"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 could not complete due to resource constraints"
}
```

## Pattern: metrics → Attach Raw Data
**When**: Have numbers but interpretation unclear
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_results": {
      "bleu_vanilla": 0.42,
      "bleu_rrp": 0.48,
      "bleu_freq_penalty": 0.45
    },
    "status": "awaiting_interpretation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 produced metrics. Interpretation TBD."
}
```

## Pattern: anomaly → Register for Investigation
**When**: Result doesn't match ANY prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "anomaly_register",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Latency spike at 4k boundary",
    "observation": "RRP showed 3x latency at exactly 4096 tokens",
    "conflicts_with": ["H1", "H2"],
    "status": "active"
  },
  "rationale": "No hypothesis predicted latency effects"
}
```

## Pattern: observation → Critique or Assumption
**When**: Qualitative insight from running experiment
**DELTA** (if it challenges framing):
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "adversarial_critique",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Metric choice confound",
    "attack": "BLEU may not capture the coherence benefits RRP provides",
    "evidence": "Running T1 revealed outputs differed in structure, not token overlap",
    "current_status": "Observed during experiment, needs investigation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 execution revealed potential metric confound"
}
```

# When Patterns Don't Fit
- If result is ambiguous between patterns → discuss in thread
- If multiple patterns apply → create multiple DELTAs
- If no pattern fits → add to anomaly_register with status: "unclassified"

# CLI Helper
```bash
# Show interpretation guidance for a pattern
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern anomaly

# Interactive mode: walk through classification and interpretation
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --interactive \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ExperimentResultType taxonomy defined
- [ ] ClassifiedResult schema defined
- [ ] experiment classify command implemented
- [ ] All interpretation patterns documented in specs/
- [ ] experiment interpret helper command
- [ ] Guidance on when to ask for help

# Relationship to Other Work
- Depends on: Execution protocol (brenner_bot-t62l)
- Uses: Existing delta-parser and artifact-merge
- Enables: Artifact evolution from experiment evidence","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:51:12.453215707-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 child tasks completed: ExperimentResult schema (1es5.1), artifact types (1es5.2), experiment encode CLI (1es5.3), golden fixtures + tests (1es5.4)","labels":["infrastructure","protocol","research-loop"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-t62l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:52:25.940633427-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:59:54.950352935-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5.1","title":"Task: Finalize ExperimentResult schema + delta mapping rules","description":"# Goal
Lock a strict, testable contract for how `ExperimentResult` attaches to the canonical artifact.

This is **encoding**, not interpretation.

---

# Inputs
- `ExperimentResult` JSON (from `experiment run` or `experiment record`)
- Operator-provided linkage:
  - which thread
  - which test (`T#`)

---

# Outputs
- One or more DELTA blocks that update the artifact deterministically.

---

# Decisions to lock
## Where do run records live in the artifact?
Preferred: extend `discriminative_tests` items with minimal optional fields, e.g.:
- `last_run_at`
- `last_exit_code`
- `last_result_path` (relative path)
- `last_summary` (short)

Avoid:
- embedding full stdout/stderr in the artifact
- inventing a sprawling “lab notebook” section

## Mapping from `test_id` to artifact item
- Option A: require `test_id` matches the artifact test `name` prefix (e.g., `T1: ...`)
- Option B: store `test_id` as an explicit field on tests going forward

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Representation documented with examples
- [ ] Clear mapping from result JSON → artifact fields
- [ ] Provenance rule: always include command + cwd + timestamps + result path
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:03:06.113946274-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:35:51.822267438-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:35:51.822267438-05:00","close_reason":"Created specs/experiment_result_encoding_v0.1.md with extended DiscriminativeTest schema, mapping rules, examples for success/failure/timeout, and error cases","labels":["experiments","protocol","results"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:03:06.115040905-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5.2","title":"Task: Extend artifact types/rendering for experiment result fields","description":"# Goal
Ensure whatever result→DELTA encoding we adopt is actually representable and renderable in the canonical artifact.

---

# Background
Current artifact schema v0.1 has 7 sections and no explicit "results" section.
If we add new fields (e.g., `actual_results` on tests), we must update:
- Type definitions (apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.ts)
- Merge behavior (applyEdit / applyAdd)
- Canonical markdown renderer (renderArtifactMarkdown)
- Linter rules if needed

---

# Non-goal
Do not create a sprawling "lab notebook" section. We want minimal, decision-relevant results.

---

# Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Chosen representation is supported end-to-end (merge + render + lint)
- [ ] No silent dropping of result fields
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:03:19.93887011-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T23:41:06.981520151-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T23:41:06.981520151-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in 176c853: Added TestLastRun, TestStatus types; extended TestItem with experiment fields; updated renderTests() for result display","labels":["artifact-schema","compiler","experiments"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:03:19.969010924-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1es5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:37:11.632809309-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
